  • Access to global networks
  • Career advancement opportunities
  • Exposure to early-growth ventures with measurable social impact

Application Deadline

The application window for the 2026 recruitment cycle is currently open.

Application Deadline: 28 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration.

Apply Here

Why Graduates Should Not Miss This Opportunity

The elea Talent Program offers far more than a traditional graduate role. It provides participants with a rare opportunity to build expertise in impact investing while contributing to solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing social and economic challenges.

Graduates who aspire to work at the intersection of finance, entrepreneurship, and social impact will find the program particularly valuable. The combination of international exposure, professional development, mentorship, and meaningful work creates an exceptional foundation for a long-term career in the impact investing sector.

For talented graduates seeking a purpose-driven career with global opportunities, the elea Talent Program 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity to learn, grow, and create lasting impact.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post elea Talent Program 2027 in South Africa: Fully Immersive Global Career Opportunity in Impact Investing for University Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a fully funded literary development opportunity designed to empower emerging African women storytellers. This prestigious residency provides selected writers with the space, mentorship, training, and publishing platform needed to refine their craft and amplify their voices on an international stage. Organized by Majesty Publishing House Africa, the program […]

The post Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a fully funded literary development opportunity designed to empower emerging African women storytellers. This prestigious residency provides selected writers with the space, mentorship, training, and publishing platform needed to refine their craft and amplify their voices on an international stage.

Organized by Majesty Publishing House Africa, the program aims to address the long-standing barriers that many African women writers face, including limited access to mentorship, publishing opportunities, and global literary networks.

This initiative culminates in a published anthology titled “New African Voices: Stories That Shape Africa’s Future”, showcasing the works of selected participants.

 

About the Africa Women Writers Residency 2026

The residency is a structured creative writing development program running in September 2026. It is designed for twelve selected emerging women writers from across Africa.

The program combines:

  • Online intensive workshops and mentorship sessions
  • Guest lectures by established African authors
  • A fully funded in-person residency experience in Nairobi, Kenya
  • Publication of selected works in a professional anthology

The program supports writers working in multiple literary genres including:

  • Fiction
  • Fantasy
  • Creative nonfiction
  • Memoir writing
  • Poetry

This initiative is not only a training program but also a publication platform aimed at elevating African women’s literary voices globally.

 

Program Structure and Format

The residency is divided into two main phases:

1. Virtual Residency (3 Weeks)

Participants will engage in:

  • Interactive writing workshops
  • One-on-one mentorship sessions
  • Literary discussions and critiques
  • Guest lectures from established authors
  • Writing development exercises focused on thematic storytelling

2. In-Person Residency (1 Week in Nairobi, Kenya)

Selected writers will travel to Nairobi for an immersive experience that includes:

  • Intensive writing and revision sessions
  • Cultural exchange activities
  • Collaborative storytelling workshops
  • Networking with African literary professionals
  • Final editing and anthology preparation

All costs are fully covered, ensuring equal access for selected participants.

 

Funding and Support Coverage

This is a fully funded residency. Selected participants will receive:

  • International and local travel coverage
  • Accommodation during the in-person residency
  • Meals throughout the program
  • Local transportation in Nairobi
  • Cultural and learning activities
  • Mentorship and publishing support

This makes it one of the most accessible and impactful writing residencies for African women writers.

 

Eligibility Criteria

The program is open to emerging women writers from selected African countries.

Eligible Countries:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • South Africa

Eligible Applicants:

Applicants must:

  • Identify as women writers
  • Be emerging writers (published or unpublished)
  • Not currently have literary representation
  • Demonstrate proficiency in English
  • Be able to commit fully to the program duration

Eligible Writing Categories:

  • Fiction
  • Poetry
  • Creative nonfiction
  • Memoir
  • Fantasy writing

 

Application Requirements

Interested applicants must submit the following:

1. Motivation Letter (250 words)

Applicants must introduce themselves and explain:

  • Their writing journey
  • Their motivation for joining the residency
  • Their goals as writers
  • Their interest in African storytelling

2. Writing Sample (Theme-Based)

Applicants must respond to the theme:

“A New Dawn for Africa”

Submission options include:

  • A short story or essay (up to 1,000 words), OR
  • One poem (up to 1,000 words), OR
  • Two poems totaling approximately 1,000 words

 

Why This Residency Matters

The Africa Women Writers Residency plays a critical role in strengthening African literature by addressing structural barriers faced by women writers.

Key benefits include:

  • Professional literary mentorship
  • Exposure to global publishing opportunities
  • Development of storytelling skills
  • Access to an African literary network
  • Publication in a curated anthology
  • Increased visibility for emerging writers

By investing in women storytellers, the program ensures that African narratives are told authentically and reach global audiences.

 

Program Location

  • In-person residency location: Nairobi, Kenya
  • Virtual participation: Open to selected candidates across eligible countries

 

Deadline

  • Application Deadline: 22 June 2026
  • Applications must be submitted via email before the deadline: residency@majestypublishingafrica.com

 

Conclusion

The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a powerful opportunity for emerging African women writers to develop their craft, gain mentorship, and achieve publication in a professional anthology. With full funding and strong editorial support, this residency offers a rare chance to transform creative passion into professional literary success.

Writers interested in participating should submit their materials to residency@majestypublishingafrica.com before the deadline.

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The European Union has launched a major funding opportunity aimed at strengthening civil society organizations and improving local governance systems while promoting sustainable local economic development in Zimbabwe. This initiative, under the EuropeAid framework, provides substantial financial support for impactful projects that empower communities, enhance accountability, and drive inclusive economic growth at the local level. […]

The post EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union has launched a major funding opportunity aimed at strengthening civil society organizations and improving local governance systems while promoting sustainable local economic development in Zimbabwe. This initiative, under the EuropeAid framework, provides substantial financial support for impactful projects that empower communities, enhance accountability, and drive inclusive economic growth at the local level.

With a total budget of €6,000,000, this action grant is one of the most significant funding opportunities available for civil society actors working in governance, democracy support, and sustainable development in Sub-Saharan Africa.

This opportunity is officially open for submission and targets organizations capable of designing and implementing high-impact, community-driven development projects.

 

About the EuropeAid Funding Programme

The initiative is funded under the European Union’s external cooperation instruments managed by EuropeAid. EuropeAid is responsible for designing and implementing development cooperation programs that support sustainable development, poverty reduction, governance reform, and institutional strengthening across partner countries.

This specific call, EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW, focuses on Zimbabwe and aligns with broader EU development priorities in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The program aims to strengthen:

  • Democratic governance at local levels
  • Capacity of civil society organizations
  • Economic resilience in communities
  • Transparency and accountability in public administration
  • Sustainable and inclusive local economies

 

Objective of the Grant

The core objective of this funding call is:

To promote civil society contributions to good local governance and sustainable local economies in Zimbabwe.

This involves supporting initiatives that enable citizens, civil society organizations, and local institutions to work together more effectively in addressing governance challenges and promoting economic opportunities.

 

Key Areas of Intervention

Successful proposals under this grant are expected to address one or more of the following priority areas:

1. Strengthening Civil Society Organizations

  • Capacity building for NGOs and community-based organizations
  • Enhancing advocacy and policy engagement skills
  • Supporting civic participation and citizen engagement
  • Promoting organizational sustainability

2. Improving Local Governance Systems

  • Enhancing transparency and accountability mechanisms
  • Strengthening public service delivery at local level
  • Supporting participatory governance processes
  • Encouraging collaboration between local authorities and civil society

3. Promoting Sustainable Local Economies

  • Supporting local entrepreneurship and SMEs
  • Encouraging inclusive economic growth models
  • Promoting green and sustainable economic practices
  • Enhancing value chains at community level

4. Social Inclusion and Community Empowerment

  • Empowering youth and women in decision-making
  • Supporting marginalized and vulnerable groups
  • Strengthening community resilience
  • Promoting equitable access to resources

 

Funding Details

This call offers significant financial support for eligible applicants:

  • Total Budget: €6,000,000
  • Type of Funding: Action Grants
  • Geographic Focus: Zimbabwe (Sub-Saharan Africa)

Grants are expected to support multi-year interventions with measurable development impact.

 

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must design projects aligned with EU development priorities and demonstrate strong implementation capacity.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Civil society organizations (CSOs)
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • International NGOs
  • Community-based organizations
  • Development-focused consortiums
  • Non-profit institutions working in governance and development

Required Capabilities:

  • Proven experience in governance or development projects
  • Strong financial and project management systems
  • Ability to operate in Zimbabwe or Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Strong monitoring and evaluation frameworks
  • Partnerships with local stakeholders

 

Expected Impact

Projects funded under this initiative are expected to deliver:

  • Improved governance transparency and accountability
  • Stronger participation of citizens in decision-making
  • Increased effectiveness of civil society organizations
  • Enhanced local economic resilience
  • Sustainable community development outcomes

 

Application Process

Interested organizations must submit their proposals through the official EU submission platform.

Steps include:

  1. Reviewing the full call document
  2. Preparing concept notes and full proposals
  3. Developing implementation frameworks
  4. Submitting applications via the EU submission service

 

Deadline

  • Submission Deadline: 21 July 2026

Late submissions will not be accepted.

 

Official Reference

  • Programme: Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Call Reference: EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW
  • Status: Open for Submission

 

Why This Grant Matters

This EuropeAid funding opportunity is critical because it strengthens the foundation of democratic governance and sustainable economic development at the grassroots level. It empowers civil society organizations to act as catalysts for change, ensuring that communities have stronger voices in shaping policies and economic opportunities.

By investing in governance and local economies, the EU aims to build long-term resilience, reduce inequality, and promote inclusive development in Zimbabwe.

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
